extraction.one_line_summary: COUSIN Jérémy won from a breakaway that survived despite Quick-Step and Lotto-Soudal chasing, helped by a late peloton crash.
tactical_patterns: Quick-Step and Lotto-Soudal chased for sprinters on unsuitable terrain, wasting energy when GC teams stayed passive.
COUSIN targeting polka-dot points in pre-broadcast phase reveals tactical value of racing before TV coverage begins.
Late peloton crash with 2km remaining gave marginal 25-second gap decisive buffer, proving breakaway timing luck matters.

rider_signals.COUSIN Jérémy: positive: won first 2018 race after only 30 racing days in 2017, showing form recovery timing
rider_signals.QUINTANA Nairo: neutral: brother Dayer crashed in descent, but no GC impact suggests family dynamic didn't affect race
rider_signals.POLITT Nils: positive: second place after bridging solo in final 15km shows diesel engine strength
rider_signals.VIVIANI Elia: negative: ranked only 28th on moderately hilly stage, exposing climbing weakness earlier than expected
